item.title: OCR-Agent: Agentic OCR with Capability and Memory Reflection
item.description: Large Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have demonstrated significant potential on complex visual understanding tasks through iterative optimization methods.However, these models generally lack effective self-correction mechanisms, making it...

item.title: LongCLI-Bench: A Preliminary Benchmark and Study for Long-horizon Agentic Programming in Command-Line Interfaces
item.description: Recent advances in AI-assisted programming have empowered agents to execute complex workflows via command-line interfaces, however, existing benchmarks are limited by short task horizons, data contamination from GitHub scraping, and a la...

item.title: Conv-FinRe: A Conversational and Longitudinal Benchmark for Utility-Grounded Financial Recommendation
item.description: Most recommendation benchmarks evaluate how well a model imitates user behavior. In financial advisory, however, observed actions can be noisy or short-sighted under market volatility and may conflict with a user's long-term goals. Treat...

item.title: On Data Engineering for Scaling LLM Terminal Capabilities
item.description: Despite rapid recent progress in the terminal capabilities of large language models, the training data strategies behind state-of-the-art terminal agents remain largely undisclosed. We address this gap through a systematic study of data...

item.title: From Perception to Action: An Interactive Benchmark for Vision Reasoning
item.description: Understanding the physical structure is essential for real-world applications such as embodied agents, interactive design, and long-horizon manipulation. Yet, prevailing Vision-Language Model (VLM) evaluations still center on structure-a...

item.title: FlowPrefill: Decoupling Preemption from Prefill Scheduling Granularity to Mitigate Head-of-Line Blocking in LLM Serving
item.description: The growing demand for large language models (LLMs) requires serving systems to handle many concurrent requests with diverse service level objectives (SLOs). This exacerbates head-of-line (HoL) blocking during the compute-intensive prefi...

item.title: QuantVLA: Scale-Calibrated Post-Training Quantization for Vision-Language-Action Models
item.description: Vision-language-action (VLA) models unify perception, language, and control for embodied agents but face significant challenges in practical deployment due to rapidly increasing compute and memory demands, especially as models scale to l...

item.title: Learning from Trials and Errors: Reflective Test-Time Planning for Embodied LLMs
item.description: Embodied LLMs endow robots with high-level task reasoning, but they cannot reflect on what went wrong or why, turning deployment into a sequence of independent trials where mistakes repeat rather than accumulate into experience. Drawing...

item.title: One-step Language Modeling via Continuous Denoising
item.description: Language models based on discrete diffusion have attracted widespread interest for their potential to provide faster generation than autoregressive models. In practice, however, they exhibit a sharp degradation of sample quality in the f...

item.title: Implicit Intelligence -- Evaluating Agents on What Users Don't Say
item.description: Real-world requests to AI agents are fundamentally underspecified. Natural human communication relies on shared context and unstated constraints that speakers expect listeners to infer. Current agentic benchmarks test explicit instructio...

item.title: TextPecker: Rewarding Structural Anomaly Quantification for Enhancing Visual Text Rendering
item.description: Visual Text Rendering (VTR) remains a critical challenge in text-to-image generation, where even advanced models frequently produce text with structural anomalies such as distortion, blurriness, and misalignment. However, we find that le...

item.title: Query-focused and Memory-aware Reranker for Long Context Processing
item.description: Built upon the existing analysis of retrieval heads in large language models, we propose an alternative reranking framework that trains models to estimate passage-query relevance using the attention scores of selected heads. This approac...

item.title: PyVision-RL: Forging Open Agentic Vision Models via RL
item.description: Reinforcement learning for agentic multimodal models often suffers from interaction collapse, where models learn to reduce tool usage and multi-turn reasoning, limiting the benefits of agentic behavior. We introduce PyVision-RL, a reinfo...

item.title: DREAM: Deep Research Evaluation with Agentic Metrics
item.description: Deep Research Agents generate analyst-grade reports, yet evaluating them remains challenging due to the absence of a single ground truth and the multidimensional nature of research quality. Recent benchmarks propose distinct methodologie...

item.title: TAPE: Tool-Guided Adaptive Planning and Constrained Execution in Language Model Agents
item.description: Language Model (LM) agents have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in solving tasks that require multiple interactions with the environment. However, they remain vulnerable in environments where a single error often leads to irrecovera...

item.title: Benchmark Test-Time Scaling of General LLM Agents
item.description: LLM agents are increasingly expected to function as general-purpose systems capable of resolving open-ended user requests. While existing benchmarks focus on domain-aware environments for developing specialized agents, evaluating general...

item.title: The Art of Efficient Reasoning: Data, Reward, and Optimization
item.description: Large Language Models (LLMs) consistently benefit from scaled Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning, but also suffer from heavy computational overhead. To address this issue, efficient reasoning aims to incentivize short yet accurate thinking...

item.title: The Diffusion Duality, Chapter II: Ψ-Samplers and Efficient Curriculum
item.description: Uniform-state discrete diffusion models excel at few-step generation and guidance due to their ability to self-correct, making them preferred over autoregressive or Masked diffusion models in these settings. However, their sampling quali...

item.title: RankEvolve: Automating the Discovery of Retrieval Algorithms via LLM-Driven Evolution
item.description: Retrieval algorithms like BM25 and query likelihood with Dirichlet smoothing remain strong and efficient first-stage rankers, yet improvements have mostly relied on parameter tuning and human intuition. We investigate whether a large lan...

item.title: OmniOCR: Generalist OCR for Ethnic Minority Languages
item.description: Optical character recognition (OCR) has advanced rapidly with deep learning and multimodal models, yet most methods focus on well-resourced scripts such as Latin and Chinese. Ethnic minority languages remain underexplored due to complex...

item.title: Adaptive Text Anonymization: Learning Privacy-Utility Trade-offs via Prompt Optimization
item.description: Anonymizing textual documents is a highly context-sensitive problem: the appropriate balance between privacy protection and utility preservation varies with the data domain, privacy objectives, and downstream application. However, existi...

item.title: Learning to Detect Language Model Training Data via Active Reconstruction
item.description: Detecting LLM training data is generally framed as a membership inference attack (MIA) problem. However, conventional MIAs operate passively on fixed model weights, using log-likelihoods or text generations. In this work, we introduce Ac...

item.title: LaS-Comp: Zero-shot 3D Completion with Latent-Spatial Consistency
item.description: This paper introduces LaS-Comp, a zero-shot and category-agnostic approach that leverages the rich geometric priors of 3D foundation models to enable 3D shape completion across diverse types of partial observations. Our contributions are...

item.title: See and Fix the Flaws: Enabling VLMs and Diffusion Models to Comprehend Visual Artifacts via Agentic Data Synthesis
item.description: Despite recent advances in diffusion models, AI generated images still often contain visual artifacts that compromise realism. Although more thorough pre-training and bigger models might reduce artifacts, there is no assurance that they...

item.title: Communication-Inspired Tokenization for Structured Image Representations
item.description: Discrete image tokenizers have emerged as a key component of modern vision and multimodal systems, providing a sequential interface for transformer-based architectures. However, most existing approaches remain primarily optimized for rec...

item.title: SIMSPINE: A Biomechanics-Aware Simulation Framework for 3D Spine Motion Annotation and Benchmarking
item.description: Modeling spinal motion is fundamental to understanding human biomechanics, yet remains underexplored in computer vision due to the spine's complex multi-joint kinematics and the lack of large-scale 3D annotations. We present a biomechani...

item.title: Generative AI and Machine Learning Collaboration for Container Dwell Time Prediction via Data Standardization
item.description: Import container dwell time (ICDT) prediction is a key task for improving productivity in container terminals, as accurate predictions enable the reduction of container re-handling operations by yard cranes. Achieving this objective requ...

item.title: Aletheia tackles FirstProof autonomously
item.description: We report the performance of Aletheia (Feng et al., 2026b), a mathematics research agent powered by Gemini 3 Deep Think, on the inaugural FirstProof challenge. Within the allowed timeframe of the challenge, Aletheia autonomously solved 6...

item.title: Untied Ulysses: Memory-Efficient Context Parallelism via Headwise Chunking
item.description: Efficiently processing long sequences with Transformer models usually requires splitting the computations across accelerators via context parallelism. The dominant approaches in this family of methods, such as Ring Attention or DeepSpeed...

item.title: Test-Time Training with KV Binding Is Secretly Linear Attention
item.description: Test-time training (TTT) with KV binding as sequence modeling layer is commonly interpreted as a form of online meta-learning that memorizes a key-value mapping at test time. However, our analysis reveals multiple phenomena that contradi...

item.title: Multi-Vector Index Compression in Any Modality
item.description: We study efficient multi-vector retrieval for late interaction in any modality. Late interaction has emerged as a dominant paradigm for information retrieval in text, images, visual documents, and videos, but its computation and storage...

item.title: PETS: A Principled Framework Towards Optimal Trajectory Allocation for Efficient Test-Time Self-Consistency
item.description: Test-time scaling can improve model performance by aggregating stochastic reasoning trajectories. However, achieving sample-efficient test-time self-consistency under a limited budget remains an open challenge. We introduce PETS (Princip...
